question: Design a `Library` class that uses aggregation to manage a collection of `Book` objects (from Q1). The `Library` class should store pointers (or references) to `Book` objects that are created and owned externally. Implement methods to `addBook(Book* book)` and `displayAllBooks()`.

test_cases.expected:
--- Current Books in Library (Inefficient Display) ---
Entry 1: Book Title: "Dune", Author Name: "Frank Herbert", Publication Year: 1965.
Entry 2: Book Title: "Foundation", Author Name: "Isaac Asimov", Publication Year: 1951.
Entry 3: Book Title: "Neuromancer", Author Name: "William Gibson", Publication Year: 1984.
--- End of Inefficient Display ---

Library is currently empty.

errors: The `displayAllBooks` method uses an old-style index-based `for` loop instead of a more modern and readable range-based for loop.

The `displayAllBooks` method relies on `Book::getBookInfo()` which uses `std::stringstream` for each book's output, creating temporary strings and potentially being less efficient than direct stream insertion.
The output formatting in `displayAllBooks` is slightly verbose and less concise than idiomatic C++ stream output.
